data.title: Bitcoin Outperforms Altcoins as Market Favors Leading Cryptocurrency
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index currently stands at 27, indicating that Bitcoin has been outperforming most altcoins over the past 90 days. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates the price performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies against Bitcoin, excluding stablecoins and wrapped tokens. A score below 75 suggests that the market is currently favoring Bitcoin over altcoins.

The organization sold 93.77 Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC), valued at $8.07 million, in exchange for 2,868.4 Ethereum (ETH) at a rate of $2,813 per ETH. The transaction was conducted from the address 0xee7f7f53f0d0c8c56a38e97c5a58e4d321a174dc, as reported by Nansen AI.

data.title: Triple Threats Loom Over Markets Amid U.S.-Canada Trade Tensions and Currency Intervention Plans
data.description: Phyrex posted on X. This week, three significant challenges have emerged that could impact global markets. Firstly, U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to impose a 100% tariff on Canadian goods, potentially prompting retaliatory measures from Canada and China. Secondly, there are plans for the United States and China to jointly intervene in Japan's currency, which may cause concern among carry trade investors. Lastly, the probability of a U.S. government shutdown on January 31 has risen to 78%, raising fears of a repeat of the October shutdown. These developments are expected to influence U.S. stock futures and the broader stock market upon opening.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Weekend Decline Amid Trade and Economic Concerns
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. Over the recent weekend, Bitcoin experienced a notable decline, exacerbated by several economic and trade concerns. U.S. President Donald Trump's dissatisfaction with a trade agreement involving Canada and China has been a focal point, with Trump issuing multiple warnings to Canada. His use of tariffs as a strategic tool has raised market apprehensions about potential retaliatory measures from Canada and China, which could lead to increased market volatility.

Additionally, there is speculation about possible U.S.-Japan intervention in the yen's exchange rate, which, while less severe than tariff issues, could impact the arbitrage market, particularly affecting yen-based lending assets. Furthermore, the probability of a U.S. government shutdown has risen, with Kalshi predicting a 78.5% chance due to budgetary issues set to expire on January 31. However, unlike the comprehensive shutdown in October last year, some government departments have secured full-year funding, potentially mitigating the impact.

Bitcoin's turnover rate increased significantly over the weekend, indicating growing market panic amid these negative factors. Investors are facing heightened uncertainty, even before the U.S. stock market opens. The upcoming hours will reveal the reactions of Asian investors, followed by those in the U.S.

The repeated use of tariffs by Trump as a weapon has historically led to unfavorable outcomes, and the market's negative response may prompt further actions. The situation remains tense as stakeholders await developments.

data.title: The Polymarket betting pool for "Gold or ETH to reach $5,000 first" has settled, with total bets totaling nearly $1.62 million.
data.description: According to Polymarket, with international gold prices breaking through $5,000/ounce, the betting pool for "Gold or ETH: Which will first reach $5,000?" has been successfully settled. Ultimately, this betting event attracted nearly $1.62 million. Previously, on January 9th, Polymarket reported that bets on "Gold or ETH: Which will first reach $5,000?" totaled nearly $840,000, with the probability of "Gold reaching $5,000 first" initially reported as 68%, and a deadline of June 30, 2026. If neither reaches $5,000 by then, settlement will be made at a 50%:50% ratio. The gold price was referenced to the COMEX gold continuous contract; the ETH price was referenced to the Binance ETH/USDT price.

data.title: Japan may lift the ban on spot cryptocurrency ETFs in 2028; SBI and Nomura have already begun product preparation.
data.description: Japan's Financial Services Agency (FSA) is studying the possibility of lifting the ban on cryptocurrency ETFs, including Bitcoin, as early as 2028. The plan includes amending the Enforcement Ordinance of the Investment Trust Law to include virtual currencies within the scope of "specific assets" that investment trusts can invest in. Reports indicate that large financial institutions such as SBI Holdings and Nomura Holdings have already begun developing related ETF products. Once approved for listing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, individual investors could potentially participate in cryptocurrency ETF investments through their securities accounts, similar to trading stocks or gold ETFs. Previous surveys showed that at least six asset management institutions are researching related products, covering both individual and institutional clients. The report also points out that a key prerequisite for lifting the ban is tax reform. Currently, Japan imposes a comprehensive tax of up to 55% on virtual asset gains, and discussions are considering adjusting this to a separate tax system of approximately 20%. Analysts believe that if the tax system and regulations are relaxed simultaneously, it will help expand the asset allocation options for individual and institutional investors. (Nikkei)

data.title: Decentralized Custody Startup Entropy Shuts Down After Four Years
data.description: Entropy, a decentralized custody startup, has announced its closure after four years of operation. According to NS3.AI, the company, which was supported by a16z and other significant investors, specialized in multi-party computation and cross-chain asset management. Despite raising approximately $27 million, Entropy determined that its business model was unable to support venture-scale growth. This decision underscores the increasing challenges faced by crypto startups since 2025.

data.title: Gold Prices Hit Record High Amid Global Economic Concerns
data.description: Gold prices have soared to an unprecedented $5,000 per ounce, reflecting heightened market anxiety. According to NS3.AI, this surge is attributed to escalating tariffs involving the US, Canada, and China, potential intervention in the yen currency, and the increased likelihood of a US government shutdown. The rise in gold prices underscores a growing distrust in the global financial system, compounded by a weakening US dollar and central banks' increased gold purchases. Investors are turning to gold as a safe haven amid uncertain macroeconomic conditions and rising geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Bitcoin OG poured an additional $20 million USDC into Hyperliquid to avoid liquidation, widening its overall unrealized losses to approximately $83.4 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, as the market declined, Bitcoin OG (10/11) deposited approximately $20 million USDC into Hyperliquid as margin to avoid liquidation of its positions. Currently, the address's overall unrealized losses have widened to approximately $83.4 million, with accumulated funding fees of approximately $8 million. Its paper profits have fallen sharply from a previous high of approximately $142.5 million to approximately $9.7 million. As of now, its main holdings are: ETH: 223,340.65 coins, worth approximately $632 million; BTC: 1,000 coins, worth approximately $86.99 million; SOL: 511,612.85 coins, worth approximately $61 million.

data.title: CME Bitcoin Futures Open with Significant Price Gap
data.description: CME Bitcoin futures began trading on Monday with a notable $2,940 price gap, opening at $86,560 after closing the previous week at $89,500. According to NS3.AI, this discrepancy arises because CME futures markets are closed over the weekend, whereas the spot Bitcoin market remains active. Investors are keenly observing whether the futures price will adjust to bridge this gap caused by the weekend closure.

data.title: In the past 12 hours, approximately $583 million in positions were liquidated across the entire network, with the largest single liquidation amounting to approximately $15 million.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, the total liquidation amount across the cryptocurrency market's futures contracts in the past 12 hours was approximately $583 million. Of this, long positions accounted for approximately $553 million, a significant portion, while short positions accounted for approximately $30.12 million. Overall, this round of liquidations primarily focused on long positions. The largest single liquidation occurred on Binance-ETHUSDT, valued at $15.2275 million.
